# FlowBoard Getting Started Guide

## Creating Your Account

Welcome to FlowBoard! Creating your account takes less than 2 minutes. Visit flowboard.io and click "Start Free Trial" in the top right. You'll need to provide:

1. Your email address (work email recommended for team invites)
2. A password (minimum 8 characters, we recommend a password manager)
3. Your workspace name (usually your company or team name)
4. Your role (Developer, Designer, Product Manager, Marketing, Other)

After submitting, check your email for a verification link. Click it to verify your email and you'll land on the FlowBoard dashboard. Your 14-day Pro trial starts immediately - no credit card required!

## Setting Up Your First Project

Once you're in FlowBoard, you'll see a "Create Project" button. Click it and you'll be prompted for:

**Project name:** Keep it descriptive but concise (e.g., "Website Redesign Q1" or "Mobile App v2.0")

**Project key:** A 2-4 letter abbreviation used for task IDs (e.g., "WEB" creates tasks like WEB-1, WEB-2). This can't be changed later.

**Template:** Choose from 20+ templates (Software Development, Marketing Campaign, Product Launch, Event Planning, etc.) or start blank. Templates pre-populate your board with common task columns and example tasks you can customize or delete.

**Privacy:** Public (visible to all workspace members) or Private (invite-only). You can change this later in Project Settings.

Click "Create Project" and you'll land on your new project board!

## Inviting Team Members

Let's get your team onboard. Click your workspace name in the top left, then "Settings" > "Team". Click "Invite Members" and enter email addresses. You can paste multiple emails separated by commas.

**Choose a role for each invite:**

- **Admin:** Full access, can manage billing, workspace settings, and delete projects
- **Member:** Can create/edit projects and tasks, can't manage billing
- **Guest:** Read-only access to specific projects (great for clients or external stakeholders)

Invited users receive an email with a link to join your workspace. They'll create their own password and land directly in your workspace. If someone doesn't receive the email, check spam folders or use the "Copy invite link" option to send it manually via Slack or other channels.

**Pro tip:** Set up your team first before creating projects. That way you can assign tasks to people right from the start.

## Creating Your First Board

Now let's create your first set of tasks. On your project page, click the "+ Add Task" button or press `C` on your keyboard.

**Task creation form:**

- **Title:** What needs to be done? (e.g., "Design homepage mockup" or "Fix login bug")
- **Description:** Optional but recommended. Use markdown for formatting, add checklists, and paste images directly.
- **Assignee:** Who's responsible? Select from your team members.
- **Priority:** Low, Medium, High, or Critical (shown with color coding)
- **Due date:** Click the calendar icon or type dates like "next Friday" or "Dec 15"
- **Status:** To Do, In Progress, In Review, or Done (these are the default columns)

Click "Create Task" or press Cmd+Enter (Mac) / Ctrl+Enter (Windows). Your task appears on the board!

**Bulk task creation:** Need to add many tasks at once? Click the "..." menu at the top right of your board and select "Import from CSV" or "Create from template". You can also paste a list of task titles and we'll create them all instantly.

## Using Views (Kanban/Timeline/List)

FlowBoard offers 4 different ways to visualize your project. Switch between them using the tabs at the top of your project.

**Kanban View** (default): Drag-and-drop cards across columns. This is the classic board view. Great for sprint planning, pipeline management, and any workflow with distinct stages. Drag tasks between columns to change their status. Drag up/down within a column to prioritize.

**Timeline View:** Horizontal Gantt chart showing tasks on a calendar. Each task is a bar spanning from start date to due date. Drag bars to reschedule, resize to change duration. See dependencies as connecting lines. Great for project planning, resource management, and identifying scheduling conflicts.

**List View:** Spreadsheet-style view showing all task details in rows. Sort by any column, filter by assignee/priority/status, and bulk-edit multiple tasks at once. Great for power users who want keyboard-driven workflows and data entry.

**Calendar View:** Monthly or weekly calendar showing tasks by due date. Great for deadline tracking and understanding team workload at a glance.

**Pro tip:** Each view remembers your filters and sort order. You might use Kanban for daily standups, Timeline for weekly planning, and List for bulk status updates.

## Tips for New Users

**Keyboard shortcuts:** Press `?` anywhere to see the full list. Key shortcuts: `C` creates tasks, `K` opens command palette, `/` searches, `E` edits selected task, `1-4` switches views.

**Filters:** Click "Filter" at the top of any view to show only tasks assigned to you, due this week, or tagged with specific labels. Combine filters for powerful queries like "High priority tasks due this week assigned to Sarah".

**Automations:** Pro users can set up rules like "When status changes to Done, post to #launches Slack channel" or "When due date is 3 days away, send email reminder". Go to Project Settings > Automations to explore.

**Mobile apps:** Download the iOS or Android app to get push notifications, create tasks on the go, and work offline. Changes sync instantly when you reconnect.

**Search everything:** Press `/` to search across all your projects. Search by task title, description, comments, or even attachment filenames. Results show snippets with your search terms highlighted.

**Templates:** Save your project as a template from Project Settings > Save as Template. Reuse it for future projects with all the same columns, automations, and structure.

**Import from other tools:** Have existing projects in Jira, Asana, or Trello? Go to Settings > Import Data and follow the wizard. We'll map your fields, import your tasks, and preserve comments and attachments.

Need help? Click the ? icon in the bottom right for live chat (Pro users) or email support@flowboard.io. Our help center at help.flowboard.io has video tutorials and step-by-step guides for every feature.
